text:
COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT
FoundationOne CDx

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PATIENT / SPECIMEN INFORMATION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Patient Name: Raymond Kowalski
Date of Birth: [REDACTED]
Ordering Physician: [Attending Oncologist, Medical Oncology]
Diagnosis (Submitted): Metastatic colorectal adenocarcinoma, KRAS G12D mutant, MSS
Specimen ID: F1CDx-RK-2B
Specimen Type: Formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) core needle biopsy tissue
Site of Biopsy: Right lower lobe pulmonary nodule (CT-guided percutaneous core needle biopsy)
Date of Procedure: [SEE CLINICAL RECORD]
Specimen Adequacy: Adequate for analysis; tumor content estimated at 35% (minimum threshold: 20%)
Comparator: Baseline primary tumor profile (sigmoid colon biopsy, F1CDx-RK-1A) available for comparison
Platform: FoundationOne CDx (F1CDx) — hybrid capture-based comprehensive genomic profiling assay
Genomic Coverage: 324 cancer-relevant genes; whole-genome copy number analysis; selected gene fusions; tumor mutational burden; microsatellite instability
Note: This specimen was obtained from a post-treatment pulmonary metastasis following exposure to mFOLFOX6/bevacizumab (12 cycles) and FOLFIRI/ramucirumab (7+ cycles) to evaluate for acquired resistance mechanisms.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
BIOMARKER SUMMARY
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB):       6 mutations/Mb  [LOW; threshold for high: ≥10 mut/Mb]
Microsatellite Status (MSI):         MSS (Microsatellite Stable) — unchanged from baseline
Genomic Loss of Heterozygosity:      16% (intermediate; unchanged from baseline ~14%)
PD-L1 Expression (IHC, 22C3):       Not assessed on this specimen

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GENOMIC FINDINGS
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

SECTION 1: ALTERATIONS RETAINED FROM BASELINE PRIMARY TUMOR PROFILING
(Tumor clonal drivers and passengers persisting through chemotherapy exposure)

Gene         | Alteration                  | AF    | Functional Class          | Interpretation
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
KRAS         | p.G12D (c.35G>A)            | 47%   | Missense — oncogenic      | PATHOGENIC — Retained dominant clonal driver. RAS pathway constitutive activator; confers resistance to all EGFR-directed therapies (cetuximab, panitumumab). Allele frequency increased modestly from baseline (42% → 47%), consistent with stable clonal dominance.
TP53         | p.R248W (c.742C>T)          | 51%   | Missense — LOF            | PATHOGENIC — Retained. Loss-of-function hotspot mutation disrupting DNA-binding domain; loss of p53 tumor suppressor activity. Clonal (AF consistent with baseline 48%).
APC          | p.E1554* (c.4660G>T)        | 48%   | Nonsense — truncating     | PATHOGENIC — Retained. Loss of tumor suppressor function via truncation upstream of the mutation cluster region; canonical colorectal adenoma-carcinoma sequence driver.
SMAD4        | p.R361H (c.1082G>A)         | 41%   | Missense — LOF            | LIKELY PATHOGENIC — Retained. TGF-beta/BMP signaling pathway alteration; associated with inferior outcomes in stage IV CRC including potential resistance to anti-VEGF therapy and increased risk of peritoneal dissemination.
CDKN2A       | Homozygous deletion         | N/A   | Copy number loss          | PATHOGENIC — Retained. Loss of p16/ARF tumor suppressor locus (9p21.3); abrogates both cell cycle G1 checkpoint (p16→CDK4/6→RB axis) and ARF-mediated p53 stabilization.
FBXW7        | p.R505C (c.1513C>T)         | 19%   | Missense — LOF            | LIKELY PATHOGENIC — Retained. E3 ubiquitin ligase component; loss of function leads to stabilization of oncoproteins including MYC, NOTCH, and cyclin E. Allele frequency slightly decreased from baseline (22% → 19%), possibly representing subclonal drift.

SECTION 2: NEW OR ACQUIRED ALTERATIONS (NOT DETECTED OR LOW-LEVEL AT BASELINE)
(Alterations consistent with clonal evolution, treatment selection, or intratumoral heterogeneity)

Gene         | Alteration                  | AF    | Functional Class          | Interpretation
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
KRAS         | p.G12V (c.35G>T)            | 11%   | Missense — oncogenic      | LIKELY PATHOGENIC — NEWLY DETECTED (subclonal). A second, distinct KRAS codon 12 mutation detected at low allele frequency. This likely represents an independent subclone not detected in the primary tumor specimen or a minor clone below prior detection threshold. KRAS G12V drives constitutive RAS/MAPK signaling; coexistence of G12D and G12V subclones is consistent with intratumoral heterogeneity and parallel RAS evolution under selective pressure. Both mutations independently exclude anti-EGFR therapy benefit.
PIK3CA       | p.E545K (c.1633G>A)         | 18%   | Missense — GOF            | LIKELY PATHOGENIC — ACQUIRED/NEWLY DETECTED. Activating hotspot in the helical domain of PI3K catalytic subunit alpha (exon 9). PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway activation; associated with treatment resistance in mCRC. Not detected at baseline (neither in primary tumor biopsy nor in baseline liquid biopsy). Allele frequency of 18% suggests subclonal acquisition, possibly selected under VEGF-pathway inhibitor exposure.
MYC          | Amplification (CN 8.2)      | N/A   | Copy number gain          | PATHOGENIC — AMPLIFIED (INCREASED from baseline CN ~3.1). MYC is located at 8q24; prior baseline profiling demonstrated 8q gain (consistent with low-level MYC locus involvement). Current specimen demonstrates high-level focal MYC amplification (CN 8.2). MYC amplification promotes transcriptional reprogramming, cell proliferation, and has been associated with resistance to chemotherapy and anti-angiogenic agents.
RB1          | p.Q494* (c.1480C>T)         | 14%   | Nonsense — truncating     | LIKELY PATHOGENIC — ACQUIRED. Truncating mutation in the retinoblastoma tumor suppressor gene; leads to loss of G1/S checkpoint function and contributes to cell cycle dysregulation. Consistent with bypass of CDKN2A-mediated CDK4/6 inhibition. Not detected at baseline. Subclonal (AF 14%).

SECTION 3: NEGATIVE FINDINGS OF CLINICAL SIGNIFICANCE
(Genes assessed and found to be wild-type or not amplified)

• BRAF: V600E not detected; non-V600E mutations not detected
• NRAS exons 2-4: Wild-type (no codon 12/13/59/61/117/146 mutations)
• ERBB2 (HER2): Not amplified (copy number 2.4); no activating mutations (S310F, L755S, V777L, V842I not detected)
• MET: Not amplified (copy number 2.1); exon 14 skipping not detected
• FGFR1/2/3/4: No amplifications; no activating mutations
• NTRK1/2/3: No fusions detected
• RET: No fusions detected
• ALK: No fusions detected
• KDR (VEGFR2): No amplification; no activating mutations detected (ramucirumab target locus unaltered)
• POLE/POLD1: Wild-type (no pathogenic mutations; consistent with low TMB and MSS phenotype)
• MLH1/MSH2/MSH6/PMS2: No somatic mutations detected (consistent with pMMR/MSS status)
• IDH1/IDH2: Wild-type
• BRCA1/BRCA2: No pathogenic alterations detected (germline relevance — formal genetic counseling recommended if clinically indicated)

SECTION 4: COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS (GENOME-WIDE)

• Chromosome 8q gain: Persistent; now with focal high-level MYC amplification (8q24) superimposed
• Chromosome 18q loss: Retained (SMAD2/SMAD4 locus — consistent with retained SMAD4 mutation)
• Chromosome 20q gain: Retained
• Chromosome 13q loss (RB1 locus): NEW — focal deletion at 13q14, consistent with RB1 p.Q494* truncating mutation (biallelic inactivation)
• Chromosome 17p loss: Retained (consistent with TP53 LOH)
• No high-level amplifications detected beyond MYC

SECTION 5: GENE FUSIONS
No gene fusions detected. Comprehensive analysis performed for NTRK1/2/3, RET, ALK, ROS1, FGFR1-4, BRAF, and NRG1 fusion partners.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ACTIONABILITY ASSESSMENT
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TIER I — FDA-APPROVED THERAPY IN THIS TUMOR TYPE / INDICATION
• None identified.
  — KRAS mutations (G12D, G12V): Exclude benefit from cetuximab/panitumumab (negative predictive biomarkers). No FDA-approved KRAS G12D-specific inhibitor is indicated for colorectal cancer at this time.
  — MSS phenotype: Patient does not meet criteria for pembrolizumab (MSI-H/dMMR) or other immune checkpoint inhibitors in first/second-line mCRC.
  — TMB-Low (6 mut/Mb): Does not meet TMB-High threshold (≥10 mut/Mb) for pembrolizumab tumor-agnostic approval.

TIER II — FDA-APPROVED THERAPY IN DIFFERENT TUMOR TYPE / INVESTIGATIONAL IN THIS INDICATION
• PIK3CA p.E545K: PI3K alpha-selective inhibitors (alpelisib) are FDA-approved in PIK3CA-mutant HR+/HER2- breast cancer; not approved in colorectal cancer. No standard role in current mCRC management. May be of interest in basket trial context.
• MYC amplification: No approved MYC-targeted therapies; under investigation (BRD4 inhibitors, CDK inhibitors as indirect MYC pathway strategies).
• RB1 loss + CDKN2A deletion: CDK4/6 inhibitors (palbociclib, ribociclib, abemaciclib) are FDA-approved in other indications; loss of RB1 may paradoxically reduce CDK4/6 inhibitor efficacy (RB1 is the effector target). No actionable implication in current setting.

TIER III — CLINICAL TRIAL ELIGIBILITY CONSIDERATIONS
• KRAS G12D mutation: Patient may be eligible for trials investigating MRTX1133 (KRAS G12D-selective inhibitor) or combination KRAS G12D inhibition strategies. Recommend enrollment on available KRAS G12D-directed clinical trial.
• KRAS G12D + PIK3CA E545K co-mutation: Concurrent RAS and PI3K pathway activation may be relevant for trials combining KRAS and PI3K inhibition.
• SHP2 inhibitors (RMC-4630, TNO155) as KRAS pathway co-targeting: Potentially relevant in RAS-mutant tumors regardless of specific allele.
• SMAD4 loss: Associated with inferior outcomes; SMAD4-loss-directed approaches under investigation in preclinical/early clinical settings.

TIER IV — BIOMARKERS OF POTENTIAL BIOLOGIC SIGNIFICANCE (NO CURRENT CLINICAL ACTIONABILITY)
• Subclonal KRAS G12V: Intratumoral heterogeneity; may emerge as dominant clone under continued selective pressure.
• RB1 loss: Cell cycle checkpoint abrogation; potential for synthetic lethality approaches under investigation.
• FBXW7 loss: MYC/NOTCH stabilization; may contribute to resistance phenotype.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERPRETATION SUMMARY
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
This post-treatment repeat biopsy specimen from a right lower lobe pulmonary metastasis demonstrates significant molecular evolution compared to the baseline primary tumor profile. The dominant clonal architecture (KRAS G12D, TP53 R248W, APC E1554*, SMAD4 R361H, CDKN2A deletion) is fully preserved, consistent with derivation from the original tumor clone.

Novel acquired alterations include: (1) a subclonal second KRAS G12V mutation suggesting parallel RAS evolution or intratumoral heterogeneity; (2) PIK3CA E545K acquisition, activating a parallel PI3K/AKT/mTOR survival pathway that may contribute to chemotherapy resistance; (3) high-level MYC amplification, representing significant genomic amplification from the low-level 8q gain seen at baseline; and (4) subclonal RB1 truncating mutation, providing an additional cell cycle checkpoint bypass.

The MSS phenotype and low TMB are unchanged, confirming that hypermutation or mismatch repair deficiency has not emerged as an acquired resistance mechanism — a relevant consideration given the FOLFIRI exposure. The ramucirumab target (KDR/VEGFR2) shows no acquired amplification or activating mutation.

Overall, this genomic profile is consistent with clonal evolution under treatment selection pressure, with activation of parallel oncogenic signaling (PI3K pathway) and enhanced proliferative drive (MYC amplification) as potential resistance mechanisms. No immediately actionable FDA-approved therapy is identified in the colorectal cancer setting. Clinical trial enrollment targeting KRAS G12D or RAS pathway co-inhibition strategies is strongly encouraged.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL NOTES
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
• Sequencing performed to a mean coverage depth of 780x (target: ≥500x).
• Allele frequencies reported reflect tumor cell fraction and may differ from true mutant allele frequency in pure tumor cells.
• Tumor content 35% (adequate); all alterations called using validated somatic-only pipeline.
• Germline alterations cannot be fully excluded without matched normal tissue; germline genetic counseling recommended for BRCA1/2, MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2 if clinical suspicion.
• Fusions detected by DNA hybrid capture with supplementary RNA-based analysis for NTRK1/2/3, RET, ALK.
• Report generated and reviewed by board-certified molecular pathologist and medical oncologist.

This report is intended for use by qualified healthcare professionals in the context of the full clinical picture. Therapeutic decisions should not be based solely on this report.
